headings: h2: WORKING HARD FOR MANCHESTER WITHINGTON
text_excerpt: Contact – JEFF SMITH MP JEFF SMITH MP WORKING HARD FOR MANCHESTER WITHINGTON Menu Skip to content Home About Parliament PMQs & Parliamentary Tours Drug Law Reform Mental Health Environment Policies Assisted Dying Bill Israel, Palestine & Lebanon Help & Advice Cost of Living Crisis Cost of Living Crisis Energy Support Debt Advice Mortgage repayments Emergency Support Finances, Social Security & Employment Housing Immigration & Asylum Local Councillors Contact Privacy Policy Your Local Election Candidates for May 2026 Cllr Suzannah Reeves – Old Moat Ward Ben Williams – Didsbury West Ward Cllr Joanna Midgley – Chorlton Park Ward Cllr John Hacking – Chorlton Ward Cllr Andrew Simcock – Didsbury East Ward Cllr Angela Gartside – Withington Ward Contact There is a strict parliamentary protocol which prevents MPs from intervening on behalf of another MP’s constituents. If it is the first time you have contacted Jeff, you will need to provide your full name and postal address, as well as e-mail address and a phone number. To find out if Jeff is your MP, please click here and enter your postcode. Before getting in contact, you may be able to find the information on the Help & Advice page useful to you, including the role of an MP and the appropriate point of contact for your concerns. How To Get In Touch With Me Please do get in touch if you’ve got a problem – or you’re from either a local organisation, a community group or business, and you think we should meet – by emailing me at jeff.smith.mp@parliament.uk or calling on 0161 445 0678. You can also use the contact form below . Please note that we will be unable to respond without any contact details provided so leave your name, e-mail and full address. ← Back Message sent Thank you for your message to Jeff Smith MP. Your email has been received. HAVE YOU INCLUDED YOUR ADDRESS DETAILS? If this is the first time you have contacted me, please be aware there is a strict parliamentary convention that MPs can only deal with matters raised by their own constituents or businesses in the constituency. If you are a Manchester Withington constituent, please ensure that you have provided the following information: · Full name · Postal address · E-mail · Contact telephone number (where possible) Members of Parliament are there to help with matters for which Parliament or central government is responsible. For example, this might be Home Office applications, HMRC tax issues, DWP benefits & pensions; or queries about government policy. If you are unsure who your local MP is, you can find out here http://findyourmp.parliament.uk/ For some matters that are more local, the appropriate first step may be to contact your local councillor. You can find out who that is here https://jeffsmithmp.com/local-councillors/ I try to reply to emails as quickly as possible but please note I receive a large number of emails, letters and phone calls every day, so I would ask for your understanding if you do not receive an immediate reply. If you copy me into an email to someone else, its content will be noted but further action may not be taken. Very rarely I may miss an email, so if you haven’t received a response after two weeks, please do not hesitate to get back in touch. When I take up casework on your behalf, it may be necessary for me or my office to share personal details that you provide such as your telephone number and email address, with government departments, local authorities, public bodies and other organisations. I will only share as much information as is necessary to progress your case. You can find out more information about how I collect and use personal data here. https://jeffsmithmp.com/2018/04/01/privacy-notice/ Thank you once again for contacting me. Website: http://www.jeffsmithmp.com Twitter: @JeffSmithetc Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/jeffsmithforwithington/ Name (required) Email (required) Phone Address (required) Message Contact Us Submitting form Δ Appointments I and my team can meet
